title: Calliandra uribei
intro: Calliandra uribei, also known as the Uríbei or Mountain Coral Tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant is rich in various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Calliandra uribei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Calliandra uribei is a species of flowering plant in the legume family, Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Calliandra, which consists of over 100 species of shrubs and small trees. The genus Calliandra is further divided into several sections, with C. uribei being part of the section Calanthae. The plant is native to Colombia, where it can be found in the departments of Antioquia and Caldas. The classification of C. uribei is as a species of Fabaceae, specifically within the subfamily Faboideae.
uses: Calliandra uribei is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Latin America. Its parts, such as the leaves and flowers, are used to treat various health issues. The plant has been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and relieve pain, and it is also believed to have antimicrobial properties. It is also used in the treatment of various gastrointestinal issues, including diarrhea and ulcers. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Calliandra uribei for medicinal purposes.
